definition:
ἄ-πιστος, -ον
 (ἀ- neg., πιστός) [in LXX: Pro.17:6 28:25, Isa.17:10 * ;] 
  (a) of things, incredible: Act.26:8; 
   (b) of persons, without faith or trust, unbelieving: Mat.17:17, Mrk.9:19, Luk.9:41 12:46, Jhn.20:27, Tit.1:15, Rev.21:8; specif., of unbelievers as opposite to Christians: 1Co.6:6 7:12-15 10:27 14:22-24, 2Co.4:4 6:14-15, 1Ti.5:8 (cf. Lft., Notes, 265; Cremer, 491).†
 (AS)
